title: rubus iodostachys
intro: Rubus iodostachys, also known as Japanese wild raspberry or Ito-gaki, is a species of bramble in the rose family that is native to Japan and parts of East Asia. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include its ability to reduce inflammation and its antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Rubus iodostachys include its potential to alleviate symptoms of colds and flu, as well as its use in treating digestive issues and skin conditions. Herbal preparations of Rubus iodostachys include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be prepared by steeping the leaves and stems of the plant in hot water or a solvent, and can be used to support overall health and well-being.
